data.title: L3Harris shares slide on disappointing earnings outlook; fed shutdown hit Q4
data.description:
Shares of L3Harris Technologies (LHX) slid as much as 2.9% Thursday after the company reported disappointing quarterly revenue and issued weaker-than-expected 2026 earnings guidance.

Management forecast full-year 2026 adjusted earnings of $11.30 to $11.50 a share, below the consensus estimate of $12.44.

Fourth-quarter revenue rose 2% from a year earlier to $5.6 billion, but below the consensus estimate of $5.76 billion. The company cited delayed contract awards tied to the late-year government shutdown, which weighed on reported sales, particularly in its space and airborne systems business.

L3Harris (LHX) supplies systems for U.S. and allied defense platforms, including electronic warfare, missile warning sensors, secure communications, avionics and space-based surveillance capabilities. Its technology is embedded in aircraft, missile defense systems, naval vessels and satellites.

Earnings beat driven by margins and cost actions

The company posted adjusted earnings of $2.86 a share, topping the Wall Street consensus estimate of $2.76 a share. Adjusted segment operating margin rose to 15.7% from 15.3% a year earlier, reflecting cost savings and portfolio actions.

Net income fell to about $299 million, or $1.59 a share, from about $453 million, or $2.37 a share, a year earlier, as lower operating income and higher unallocated corporate costs weighed on results.

Fourth-quarter operating margin declined to 7.0% from 10.3% in the prior year, driven by higher corporate expenses and weaker performance at Aerojet Rocketdyne following a non-cash impairment charge.

Government shutdown pressures results

Revenue growth was modest across most segments, with the company noting that delayed awards tied to the government shutdown pushed expected sales out of the quarter. L3Harris (LHX) said those delays affected both fourth-quarter and full-year revenue, primarily in space and airborne systems.

Operating income for the quarter fell to $393 million from $569 million a year earlier, while segment operating income declined slightly year over year.

CEO comments and outlook

“2025 was a clear inflection point for L3Harris,” Chief Executive Christopher Kubasik said in a statement, citing record orders and improved cash flow, while acknowledging the impact of delayed customer awards late in the year.

Looking ahead, L3Harris (LHX) forecast 2026 revenue of $23.0 billion to $23.5 billion, in line with the consensus estimate of $23.3 billion.

data.title: Blackstone signals acceleration in deal cycle and projects strong 2026 inflows with $200B dry powder
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Blackstone Inc. (BX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Stephen Schwarzman, Chairman and CEO, highlighted “the best results in our 40-year history, with distributable earnings of $1.75 a share,” capping a record year with distributable earnings rising 20% to $5.57 per share or $7.1 billion. Inflows reached $71 billion in the fourth quarter, the highest level in 3.5 years, and $240 billion for the full year. Fundraising in private wealth increased 53% year-over-year in 2025 to $43 billion. Schwarzman indicated, “our fundraising success lifted assets under management, 13% year-over-year to a new industry record of nearly $1.3 trillion.”
  • Schwarzman emphasized Blackstone’s growing market share in private wealth, citing “an estimated 50% share of all private wealth revenue across the major alternative firms.” He reported that Blackstone generated “outstanding investment performance overall, for our limited partners again in 2025, highlighted by notable strength in infrastructure, corporate private equity, our multi-asset investing business, BXMA.” The CEO identified digital infrastructure, private credit, life sciences, and specific regional exposure to India and Japan as key thematic areas for investment.
  • Schwarzman stated, “We have one of the largest IPO pipelines in our history, reflecting a diverse mix of sectors and geographies.” He noted, “Medline was Blackstone’s fourth IPO globally since last summer, and our momentum continues to build.”
  • Jonathan Gray, President and COO, described “3 powerful dynamics coming together: first, the deal environment has reached escape velocity… second, the AI revolution is creating generational opportunities to invest private capital at scale… third, adoption of private markets continues to deepen across all 3 of our major customer channels.” He highlighted Blackstone’s scale in infrastructure, credit, and private wealth, and noted, “our infrastructure platform grew a remarkable 40% year-over-year to $77 billion.”
  • Gray reported that the insurance channel’s AUM grew 18% to $271 billion, and private wealth AUM grew 16% to over $300 billion, with BCRED leading the way. He also stated, “We expect 2026 to be our busiest year in yet terms of product launches as we stated previously.”
  • Michael Chae, CFO, stated, “The fourth quarter represented the best quarter of distributable earnings per share in the firm's history.” He reported, “FRE… reached $1.5 billion in Q4 or $1.25 per share. Management fees increased 11% year-over-year to a record $2.1 billion.”

Outlook

  • Chae said, “We expect management fees to continue on a strong positive trajectory in 2026, underpinned by robust growth in the private equity credit and insurance and multi-asset investing segment with real estate management fees consistent with Q4 levels in the near term.”
  • He added, “the continued expansion of our platform perpetual capital strategies… is widening the aperture for generating fee-related performance revenues.”
  • Chae indicated, “we expect a strong year ahead, particularly with respect to our drawdown fund business with activity building as we move through the year. Overall, our embedded value and realization potential are significant, and we are very optimistic in the multiyear outlook for this.”

Financial Results

  • Blackstone reported GAAP net income for the quarter of $2 billion. Distributable earnings were $2.2 billion or $1.75 per common share. Dividend declared was $1.49 per share.
  • Fee-related earnings (FRE) reached $1.5 billion in Q4. Management fees set a record at $2.1 billion, supported by 10% growth in base management fees and a 27% increase in transaction and advisory fees.
  • Net realizations increased 59% year-over-year to $957 million, the highest level in 3.5 years. Gross performance revenues exceeded $1 billion in the quarter, with notable items including the sale of a stake in Legence, city center complex on the Las Vegas Strip, and the sale of the firm's 6% stake in Resolution Life.
  • Infrastructure funds appreciated 8.4% in the quarter and 24% for the year. Corporate private equity funds appreciated 5% in the quarter and 14% for the year. BXMA delivered a 4.3% gross return in Q4 and 13% for 2025. Real estate values appreciated approximately 1% in Q4 and 1.5% for the year.

Q&A

  • Craig Siegenthaler, BofA Securities: Asked about the IPO pipeline and sector focus. Jonathan Gray responded that IPOs would be “mostly concentrated in the corporate space,” with a focus around energy, electricity, and significant activity in the U.S. and India. He said, “it gives the investors more capital in their pockets to redeploy. So it's a very good virtuous cycle for us, and we're excited to see the IPO market coming back like it is.”
  • Michael Cyprys, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about AI deployment across the portfolio. Gray replied, “It's still early days, but we are starting to see some real impact,” especially in software engineering and data summarization, with productivity gains expected.
  • William Katz, TD Cowen: Asked about retail opportunities as rates decline. Gray explained, “the appeal of, let's say, private credit is not just about absolute returns. It's also very much around relative returns and the return premium we can generate.”
  • Alexander Blostein, Goldman Sachs: Asked about direct lending sentiment. Gray said, “On the institutional side, way they're looking at the fundamentals… their confidence in what we're doing… is going up.” He added, “In terms of sort of outlook, we'll have to wait and see on the redemption front, over time. But I think performance will be a key driver here.”
  • Glenn Schorr, Evercore: Asked about management fee ramp. Chae replied, “We feel really good about our positioning… you will see that upward ramp in contribution in the course of the year and then full year contribution next year.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for details on IPO pipeline, product innovation, fee growth, and AI strategy, with a tone that was largely positive but probing, especially around growth drivers and market share sustainability.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout both prepared remarks and Q&A. Schwarzman stated, “I couldn't have more confidence in the firm and our prospects for continued growth.” Gray and Chae echoed optimism, with Gray noting, “we have a lot of confidence in the portfolio and the outlook over time despite these headlines.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment has shifted to a more upbeat, growth-oriented stance, especially as the deal cycle accelerates and IPO/M&A momentum builds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management guidance language has shifted from cautious optimism to strong confidence, with explicit statements about accelerating deal activity, IPO pipeline, and fundraising momentum.
  • The Q4 call emphasized the record-breaking distributable earnings and inflows, as well as the strong performance in infrastructure and private wealth fundraising, compared to Q3’s focus on resilience and platform expansion.
  • Analysts in Q4 focused more on forward guidance, deployment opportunities, and product innovation, with less emphasis on credit risk concerns that were more pronounced in the previous quarter.
  • Key metrics such as assets under management, distributable earnings, and net realizations all posted sequential growth.
  • Management’s tone was more assertively optimistic in Q4, while analysts appeared increasingly interested in structural growth drivers and scalability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged “geopolitical uncertainties that are impacting markets,” but emphasized portfolio diversification and proprietary data insights as mitigating factors.
  • Gray noted that, “despite the external noise today in private credit, facts do matter and our portfolio overall is in excellent shape.”
  • Some analysts raised concerns about retail investor flows, direct lending redemptions, and the pace of recovery in real estate, with management responding that performance and product breadth remain key mitigants.

Final Takeaway

Blackstone’s fourth quarter call highlights record distributable earnings, robust inflows, and accelerating deal activity, positioning the firm for strong growth in 2026. Management underscores its leadership in private wealth and infrastructure, an expanding IPO pipeline, and readiness to deploy nearly $200 billion in dry powder. With optimism about product innovation and market opportunities across institutions, insurance, and individual investors, Blackstone signals confidence in continued momentum and value creation for shareholders.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SoFi Q4 earnings preview: 2026 EBITDA guidance, capital allocation to keep a watch on
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI) is scheduled to announce Q4 2025 earnings results on Friday, January 30th, before market open.

The consensus EPS estimate is $0.11 (+120.0% Y/Y) and the consensus revenue estimate is $982.39M (+33.8% Y/Y).

Over the last 2 years, SOFI has beaten EPS estimates 75% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 100% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 5 upward revisions and 0 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 11 upward revisions and 2 downward.

"We see downside risk to 2026 EBITDA guidance as SoFi will need to invest to scale lending and recently launched financial services products (e.g., Crypto, Options, SoFi Plus, etc.)," said J.P. Morgan in a research note.

The investment bank noted that the 2-year and 5-year Treasury yields increased ~10 basis points and ~20 basis points, respectively, since the company's Q3 earnings call. This generally decreases demand for personal loans and creates a modest headwind to earnings.

On a positive note, the investment bank said management "tends to guide conservatively on its Q4 call, and gradually raise throughout the year".

The San Francisco-based marketplace lender is trading ~7% below its 20-day simple moving average heading into its Q4 earnings release.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system grade SOFI as Hold. Meanwhile, SA authors see the stock as Buy.

"Last year, the company announced two equity rounds," noted SA contributor Deep Value Investing. "Sell-side analysts were not particularly excited about this equity raise."

Bank of America resumed coverage of SoFi Technologies with an Underperform rating. The research note suggested that the recent equity rounds could be in anticipation of an M&A wave, said the author.

"In my view, the $3B from the equity rounds may be intended to support the momentum in originations and hold more loans on the balance sheet," noted Deep Value Investing.

"I strongly believe this topic will be discussed in the upcoming earnings release, particularly in the Q&A, so keep an eye out for any updates," said the author.

Notably, peer LendingClub was trading ~13% lower during Thursday afternoon trading even as the marketplace lender delivered a better-than-expected Q4 profit and sales on strong originations growth.

data.title: Apple buys Israeli startup Q.AI for about $2B - FT
data.description:
Apple (AAPL) acquired Israeli startup Q.AI at close to a $2 billion valuation.

Patents filed by Q.AI indicate its technology being used in headphones or glasses, using "facial skin micro movements," to communicate without talking, according to a Financial Times report  on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter. 

Johny Srouji, Apple’s senior vice-president of hardware technologies, said Q.AI is “a remarkable company that is pioneering new and creative ways to use imaging and machine learning," according to the report. 

The acquisition is designed to help Apple (AAPL) narrow its gap with Meta (META), Google (GOOGL) and OpenAI (OPENAI) in the race to create new kinds of wearable devices that speak to AI.

data.title: Gran Tierra Energy rallies after achieving record monthly production in December
data.description:
Gran Tierra Energy (GTE) +7.6% in Thursday's trading to a 52-week high after reporting average production of 48,235 boe/day for December 2025, the highest monthly production in the company's history.

In Ecuador, Gran Tierra (GTE) said it reached a daily production rate of 10K bbl/day of oil in Q4, with current production rates in Ecuador standing at ~8,800 bbl/day, and has fulfilled all exploration commitments in the country, highlighted by discoveries at Conejo in the Hollín and Basal Tena sands, which delivered combined initial production rates of 3,238 bbl/day over a 60-day period.

Based on preliminary estimates, Gran Tierra (GTE) said its total average production was 46.5K boe/day in Q4 and 45.8K boe/day for the full year.

The company noted Q4 results were hurt by an inventory build of ~291K barrels of oil in Ecuador, which were sold in early January for ~$15M.

Roth Capital nevertheless downgraded Gran Tierra (GTE) to Neutral from Buy with a C$7.50 price target, saying that due to "significant risks" from high debt levels and socio-political risks, it looks for lower share prices or an improved outlook before again recommending the stock, noting that stock has surged 40% since mid-December

data.title: Microsoft leads software stocks lower; suffers 7th largest drop in company history
data.description:
Enterprise software stocks across the board were plunging on Thursday, led by Microsoft (MSFT), which was in the midst of its seventh-largest single-day drop in the company's storied history.

Microsoft had plummeted 12% by early afternoon trading. If it holds, it will rank as the most significant sell-off since March 16, 2020, when it tanked 14.7% during the COVID-19 epidemic. Before that, one has to look all the way back to April 24, 2000, when it plunged 15.6%. That month featured a double whammy after a U.S. district judge ruled that Microsoft had violated the Sherman Antitrust Act. That was also when the dot-com bubble began to burst.

Today's plunge has also wiped away nearly $400B in Microsoft's market capitalization. If it holds, it will be the second-largest single-day decline for a company in U.S. stock market history. The largest occurred last January when Nvidia (NVDA) plunged 17%, erasing nearly $600B in market cap.

Microsoft's market cap was down to $3.16T on Thursday. In July 2025, Microsoft had become only the second company to ever reach the exclusive $4T market cap club. Nvidia also reached a $4T market cap that month. Today, it is at $4.6T.

Microsoft's historic drop today came on the heels of a second-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings report that appeared positive by nearly every metric, according to most financial analysts.

However, Microsoft did indicate it was facing some capacity restraints related to AI demand. What's more, remaining performance obligations reached $625B, with about 45% of that coming from its strategic partner OpenAI (OPENAI).

"Approximately 45% of our commercial RPO balance is from OpenAI," said Microsoft Chief Financial Officer Amy Hood during Wednesday's earnings call. "The significant remaining balance grew 28% and reflects ongoing broad customer demand across the portfolio."

"But the 45% concentration in a single name, especially a company like OpenAI that is cash-burning and reliant on continuous funding to keep going, is a risk that I believe should not be ignored," said Seeking Alpha analyst Hunting Alpha. "This is especially since there is greater scrutiny on the ROI of massive AI-related spends without profitability in the current environment."

The massive buildout of AI infrastructure has also led to lower gross profit margins, which have declined from 72% to 67% over the past five quarters.

Other software stocks demonstrating significant declines on Thursday included ServiceNow (NOW), which had also dropped 12%, and Varonis Systems (VRNS), which had fallen 10%. ServiceTitan (TTAN) had declined 10%, Workday (WDAY) had declined 9%, Datadog (DDOG) had sunk 8.5%, Atlassian (TEAM) had plunged 12%, and SAP (SAP) had tanked 15%. HubSpot (HUBS) had also plummeted 12%.

data.title: Former Federal Reserve Vice Chairman Ferguson: The Fed will stand pat for the next few meetings
data.description:
Former Federal Reserve Vice Chairman Roger Ferguson expects the central bank to “stand pat for a few meetings” following their decision to hold interest rates steady after three consecutive cuts last year.

In an interview with CNBC, Ferguson noted that the Fed is now in a period of observation, watching how the economy performs before making any further moves.

Ferguson pointed to a significant upgrade in the Fed’s tone regarding the current economy, particularly highlighting the use of the word “stabilization” to describe the labor market.

This shift in language marks a change from previous meetings where employment was an area of great concern, suggesting Fed officials are gaining confidence in the job market’s footing.

While Chairman Jay Powell declined to comment on future rate decisions, Ferguson believes the central bank is signaling a neutral stance rather than preparing for rate hikes.

“I don’t think there’s a strong bias one way or the other, but I don’t think they’re ready yet to talk about a pivot to raising rates,” Ferguson said.

The conversation also addressed how artificial intelligence and automation are beginning to reshape the workforce even as the broader economy remains strong.

Ferguson described the current moment as the “leading edge of an AI productivity impact,” where companies are experimenting with the technology while job growth remains stable but not dramatic. He cautioned against reading too much into individual layoff announcements but acknowledged the trend is emerging.

On the Fed’s dual mandate of managing inflation and employment, Ferguson noted that inflation remains “somewhat elevated,” which will keep policymakers in a holding pattern. He argued that the Fed must wait to see how productivity improvements from AI affect inflation before considering further rate cuts to support labor markets.

Ferguson also praised Chairman Powell’s handling of political pressures, noting his decision to avoid commenting on his future term on the board.

“He has comported himself with quite a bit of grace,” he said, while focusing on matter-of-fact economic assessments rather than political distractions.

More on interest rates

  • Federal Reserve hits pause, after three-meeting cutting streak
  • Current Fed rate is appropriate to support goals: Powell press conference
  • Markets indecisive, turn mixed as Fed pauses interest rate cuts on Wednesday
  • Investors should expect two rate cuts by June – strategist

data.title: Caterpillar anticipates sales growth near top of 5%-7% range in 2026 driven by record $51B backlog
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Joseph Creed highlighted that "our Centennial year marked a significant milestone, and we achieved full year sales and revenues of $67.6 billion, the highest in Caterpillar's history." Creed emphasized robust MP&E free cash flow of $9.5 billion, deployment of $7.9 billion to shareholders, and a record backlog of $51 billion, up 71% year-over-year. He stated that "sales and revenues were $19.1 billion, an all-time record for a single quarter," with an 18% increase versus the previous year, driven by higher volumes across all segments. Volume growth was especially notable in Power and Energy, where sales to users rose 37% and power generation grew 44%.
  • Creed reported that full-year adjusted operating profit margin was 17.2% and adjusted profit per share was $19.06. He noted that the company ended 2025 with 827 autonomous haul trucks, up from 690 in 2024, and that power generation sales exceeded $10 billion, growing over 30% year-over-year.
  • He introduced the 2026 outlook: "we anticipate full year sales and revenues to grow around the top of the 5% to 7% long-term compound annual growth rate target," backed by the record backlog. Approximately 62% of the backlog is expected to deliver in the next 12 months.
  • The company showcased new technology at CES 2026, including the Cat AI Assistant, and pledged $25 million to workforce development.
  • CFO Andrew R. Bonfield stated: "Sales and revenues of $19.1 billion reflected an 18% increase versus the prior year. As Joe noted, this was an all-time quarterly record. Adjusted operating profit was $3.0 billion, and our adjusted operating profit margin was 15.6%. We generated strong MP&E free cash flow of $3.7 billion in the quarter and $9.5 billion for the full year."

Outlook

  • Creed projected that full-year 2026 sales and revenues will grow "around the top of the 5% to 7% CAGR target on higher volume and favorable price realization." All three segments are expected to benefit from approximately 2% positive price realization and continued growth in services revenues. Full-year adjusted operating profit margin should "exceed 2025 levels but remain near the bottom of the target range" due to ongoing tariffs and investment in growth strategy. MP&E free cash flow is expected to be slightly lower than 2025, reflecting increased capital expenditures. Capital expenditures are projected at $3.5 billion, driven by capacity expansion.
  • Bonfield stated that incremental tariff costs are expected to be around $2.6 billion in 2026, $800 million higher than 2025, and that "if we did not take the actions we plan to take in 2026, this bill will be around 20% higher."

Financial Results

  • Sales and revenues for Q4 were $19.1 billion. Adjusted operating profit was $3.0 billion, with an adjusted operating profit margin of 15.6%.
  • Adjusted profit per share in Q4 was $5.16. For the full year, adjusted profit per share reached $19.06. MP&E free cash flow for the year totaled $9.5 billion.
  • Construction Industries sales increased 15% in Q4 to $6.9 billion, with profit of $1.0 billion and margin at 14.9%, affected by a 600 basis points tariff impact. Resource Industries sales rose 13% to $3.4 billion, with profit of $360 million and margin at 10.7%, pressured by tariffs. Power and Energy sales jumped 23% to $9.4 billion, with profit of $1.8 billion and margin expanding to 19.6%.
  • The company returned $7.9 billion to shareholders in 2025 through share repurchases and dividends.

Q&A

  • Mircea Dobre, Robert W. Baird: Asked about backlog visibility and margin management with cost and tariffs. Creed explained the record $51 billion backlog and said, "for those type of orders, we have frame agreements for a lot of customers. Those will have inflationary indices tied in there for pricing."
  • Michael Feniger, BofA Securities: Inquired about the 50 gigawatt power target and capacity risk. Creed responded that capacity planning considers all industries, not just data centers, and aims for service growth with prime power and gas compression.
  • David Raso, Evercore ISI: Questioned why sales growth guidance did not match order momentum. Bonfield clarified that the sales guide is based on current capacity, and that a $1 billion-plus dealer inventory build in Q1 2026 is a key factor.
  • Tami Zakaria, JPMorgan: Asked about the battery energy storage mix in the AIP order. Creed said, "most of that order is going to be in generators and natural gas generators," with batteries a small portion.
  • Charles Albert Dillard, Bernstein: Asked if Power & Energy revenue momentum could continue at 2025's 30% growth rate. Creed emphasized, "it's not a demand issue for us. It's really going to be, can we bring on supply faster."
  • Jamie Cook, Truist Securities: Asked about backlog growth sustainability and market share gains. Creed said, "we outpaced the industry in CI. I think we are definitely a market leader in power and energy."
  • Jerry Revich, Wells Fargo: Inquired about turbine business and capacity ramp. Creed said, "solar had a record year in 2025. We expect something comparable in 2026."
  • Robert Wertheimer, Melius Research: Asked about the Monarch data center project and efficiency. Creed discussed configuring solutions to customer needs, using waste heat for cooling, and a robust quoting pipeline.
  • Kristen Owen, Oppenheimer: Sought demand drivers in Construction Industries. Creed highlighted North American strength, data center build-out, and merchandising programs supporting market share gains.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on backlog strength, capacity constraints, and margin sustainability, often pressing on risk of overcapacity, pricing, and competitive positioning, reflecting a slightly positive but cautious tone based on the extraordinary backlog and order flow.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, frequently referencing record results, strong execution, and robust demand, but acknowledged supply constraints and tariff pressures. Creed repeatedly highlighted market leadership and strategic investments, showing confidence in future growth. During Q&A, management provided detailed explanations, with little defensiveness or hesitation.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained confident, while analysts' questions shifted more toward sustainability of growth and backlog conversion.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language in Q4 2025 emphasized growth near the top of the 5%-7% CAGR, supported by a $51 billion backlog, while the previous quarter called for modest growth and improved outlook.
  • Strategic focus shifted to capacity expansion and technology investment, especially in response to rising Power & Energy demand and data center infrastructure.
  • Analysts' focus moved from understanding backlog drivers to scrutinizing backlog conversion, margin pressures from tariffs, and ability to meet demand.
  • Key metrics rose sharply: backlog climbed from $39.8 billion in Q3 to $51 billion in Q4, quarterly revenues hit $19.1 billion from $17.6 billion previously, and Power & Energy sales surged.
  • Management's confidence increased, with a greater focus on technology and services expansion.
  • Analysts' sentiment remained cautiously optimistic but more focused on potential overcapacity and margin risks.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited $2.6 billion in expected incremental tariff costs for 2026, up $800 million year-over-year, with mitigation actions underway but facing limits.
  • Capacity constraints could cap sales in Power and Energy despite strong demand, and the ability to ramp supply and bring new capacity online is critical.
  • Analysts raised concerns about overcapacity if data center demand slows, the risk of backlog conversion delays, and the impact of tariffs on margins, with management emphasizing inflation escalators and supply planning.

Final Takeaway

Caterpillar delivered record sales, revenues, and backlog in 2025, with strong volume growth in Power and Energy and continued strength in Construction Industries. Management projects 2026 sales and revenues growth around the top of the 5%-7% range, supported by a robust $51 billion backlog. Margin performance will be shaped by higher tariffs and capacity investments, but the company remains confident in its ability to deliver profitable growth and sustain market leadership across its core segments.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Landmark Bancorp signals continued expansion with net interest margin rising to 4.03% amid strong deposit growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Landmark Bancorp (LARK) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Abigail Wendel, President and CEO, stated that "Landmark's strong fourth quarter results capped off a year of outstanding revenue growth, increased profitability, pricing discipline and per share increases in earnings and tangible book value." She reported net income of $4.7 million or diluted earnings per share of $0.77 for the fourth quarter, and tangible book value increased to $20.79 per share, an increase of $0.83 versus the prior quarter.
  • Wendel highlighted that revenue growth was driven by "continued expansion in net interest income, which increased each quarter throughout 2025." She noted that the net interest margin increased 58 basis points to 3.86% for the full year, with a fourth-quarter total cost of deposits at 1.50%. She added, "Our efficiency ratio improved to 62.7% in 2025 from 69.1% in 2024 as we controlled expense growth while enhancing our capabilities."
  • Wendel announced the board declared a cash dividend of $0.21 per share to be paid February 26, 2026, representing the 98th consecutive quarterly cash dividend.
  • Mark Herpich, CFO, stated, "Net income in the fourth quarter of 2025 totaled $4.7 million compared to $3.3 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, mainly due to continued growth in net interest income." He added, "Net interest income totaled $14.8 million, an increase of $695,000 compared to the third quarter of 2025, driven by increased asset yields and lower funding costs." Herpich highlighted deposit growth, with deposits totaling $1.4 billion at year-end and increasing by $63.4 million in the quarter.
  • Raymond McLanahan, Chief Credit Officer, reported that the commercial real estate portfolio grew by $4.7 million and the agricultural portfolio by $2.9 million in the quarter. He also noted, "Nonperforming loans, primarily nonaccrual loans decreased slightly from the prior quarter, totaled just under $10 million or 0.90% of gross loans. Compared to year-end 2024, we reduced nonperforming loans by $3.1 million, an improvement of 24%."

Outlook

  • Wendel commented, "As we move into 2026, we look forward to building on the foundation set in 2025. We will continue to invest in our talented associates, make strategic investments to better serve our customers and capitalize on growth opportunities in our markets."
  • Management reiterated a disciplined approach to pricing and deposit strategy, positioning for continued growth in core markets.
  • No explicit forward-looking EPS or revenue guidance was provided for 2026 in the transcript.

Financial Results

  • Net income for Q4 2025 was $4.7 million, with diluted EPS of $0.77. Tangible book value increased to $20.79 per share. For the full year, net income reached $18.8 million or $3.07 per share.
  • Net interest margin on a tax equivalent basis improved 20 basis points to 4.03% in Q4 2025 compared to Q3 2025.
  • Net interest income reached $14.8 million in Q4 2025, an increase of $695,000 from the previous quarter. Interest income on loans was $17.9 million, while average loan balances decreased by $2.1 million.
  • Allowance for credit losses ended at $12.5 million, representing 1.12% of gross loans. Net charge-offs for Q4 were $341,000, down from $2.3 million in the prior quarter.
  • Noninterest income totaled $3.9 million, down $169,000 sequentially, primarily due to a $101,000 loss on the sale of investment securities.
  • Noninterest expense rose to $12.3 million, up $1.0 million due to higher compensation and benefits, professional fees, and an impairment loss on repossessed assets.
  • Deposits totaled $1.4 billion at year-end, up $63.4 million for the quarter. Loan-to-deposit ratio was 79.1% at December 31, 2025. Stockholders’ equity increased to $160.6 million.

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE. The transcript does not contain a Q&A session or analyst questions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' sentiment cannot be assessed as there were no analyst questions in the call.
  • Management maintained a positive and confident tone throughout the call, as reflected by Wendel’s statement: "We are pleased to report net income of $4.7 million or diluted earnings per share of $0.77 for the fourth quarter." Herpich’s remarks also projected confidence in the company’s financial position and outlook.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained consistently positive, emphasizing improvements in profitability, asset quality, and capital position.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management’s outlook remains focused on disciplined growth and investment in talent, with no major shift in strategy from Q3 to Q4 2025.
  • The efficiency ratio increased to 62.7% from 60.7%, reflecting slightly higher expense growth in Q4. Net interest margin improved to 4.03% in Q4 from 3.83% in Q3.
  • Nonperforming loans decreased both sequentially and year-over-year, with management emphasizing ongoing credit quality monitoring and risk reduction.
  • Analyst engagement remained static, with no Q&A in either quarter.
  • Management’s confidence level was sustained, highlighting continued positive operating leverage and strong capital metrics.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged ongoing monitoring of nonperforming loans and credit quality, with McLanahan stating, “We continue to proactively monitor the overall credit quality of our loan portfolio to identify emerging risks and minimize future losses.”
  • The company referenced a decrease in noninterest income due to a loss on securities sales and higher noninterest expense tied to compensation, professional fees, and impairment on repossessed assets.
  • No new external risk factors or macroeconomic concerns were highlighted beyond disciplined credit and liquidity management.

Final Takeaway

Landmark Bancorp closed 2025 with solid growth in net interest income, improved net interest margin, and a strengthened capital base, while maintaining disciplined expense management and credit quality. The company’s leadership reiterated its commitment to investing in talent and pursuing growth opportunities in its markets, signaling confidence in building upon the momentum established in the past year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Ethos Technologies shares open at IPO price amid risk-on sentiment in markets
data.description:
Ethos Technologies (LIFE) stock opened at $19.00 in its trading debut on Thursday, the same as its initial offering price. Within minutes it fell as much as 15%. To be sure, the public debut occurred as overall markets were in the red on investors' concerns about how long it will take for tech companies to see AI pay off. The Nasdaq dropped 1.4% in midday trading.

Ethos CEO Peter Colis said the company based its decision to go public more on its progress than on external factors, such as the equity markets.

The company has logged more than 50% growth in each of the last three years and is profitable, he pointed out. Ethos (LIFE) is at a scale and on a growth trajectory that "public markets were very excited," he told Seeking Alpha in an interview.

Ethos's business is based on compressing the amount of time it takes to buy life insurance — from a five- to 10-week purchase process to 10 minutes online. And it compresses insurance agents' selling process and frees them up from shepherding clients through a complicated process.

Furthermore, there's much more room for expansion in both the direct-to-consumer and the third-party, or agent, market, Colis said. "We're still in the early innings of both of those distribution models." Some 90% of life insurance is sold through agents, and Ethos only has a single-digit penetration, he added.

On the agent side of the business, Ethos (LIFE) had 10,000 agents that sold on its platform in 2024, "and that business has continued to grow."

"This is the same kind of trajectory that the auto insurance industry went through, where GEICO and Progressive were able to make it a simple online process, and that led to a lot of net new growth," he said.

Colis also highlighted the advantages of building its own digital native platform. Its competitors are either using legacy platforms or third-party vendor-managed technology,  Colis said.

He's also confident in the company's ability to harness artificial intelligence to propel its business. "Ethos is a data machine," Colis said, "and it lends itself very well to the utilization of machine learning and AI overall. It's in our DNA. It gives us a structural cost advantage that competitors cannot easily replicate."
